design_philosophy.core_loop: Observe NPC patterns and environment, plan a non-lethal infiltration route using stealth, light/sound awareness, and environmental manipulation, execute the robbery objective while remaining undetected
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk of detection through visual and auditory exposure; tension between player agency in approach versus environmental constraints and NPC vigilance
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master thief executing careful heists through cunning and observation rather than combat prowess; infiltrator who controls the situation through knowledge of the world
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical rejection of combat-first FPS design in favor of non-confrontational first-person gameplay. Game explicitly validates stealth, evasion, and distraction over direct confrontation. Uses light and sound as core mechanical systems rather than visual obstacles. Emergent, largely unscripted level design rewards player experimentation and environmental awareness. Named 'first-person sneaker' to distinguish from shooters. Challenges player to avoid rather than engage hostile NPCs.

difficulty.continues_system: Mission-based; failing key objectives or death requires replay or loading previous save
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-mission checkpointing; no mid-mission saves implied
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty settings changeable between missions. Higher difficulties add constraints: no-kill requirements, loot theft quotas, reduced player health, increased NPC environmental sensitivity. Player can customize challenge per mission.

progression.upgrade_system: Equipment-based; blackjack and other tools available, but limited combat proficiency by design
progression.unlock_mechanism: Mission-based linear progression; completion of objectives required to advance
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty settings per mission alter objectives and NPC behavior
Emergent gameplay from unscripted levels encourages replaying with different approaches
Loot quotas on higher difficulties incentivize thorough exploration
No-kill constraints on hardest difficulties fundamentally change mission parameters

world_building_techniques: NPC dialogue and patrol patterns establish social hierarchy and faction tensions
Environmental storytelling through level design (architectural styles, guarded areas, servant vs guard zones)
Cutscenes framing mission context and plot advancement
Radio/ambient NPC conversations hint at larger plot and world events
Mission briefings establish objectives within narrative context

faction_indicators: Guard vs servant vs non-human NPC categories with distinct behavioral responses
Social hierarchy evidenced by NPC roles and patrol patterns
Guarded facilities vs servant quarters vs public spaces establish territorial control
danger_signals: NPC alert states based on suspicion levels (brief ignorance to active searching)
Visual detection meter (gem HUD indicator) shows player visibility gradient
Audio cues from guard calls, alarm reactions, and NPC communication of threats
Environmental markers of danger: blood stains, opened doors, fallen bodies trigger NPC responses
environmental_storytelling: Architectural design of levels reflects faction control and social structure
Lighting design creates narrative about surveillance and security
Sound design establishes NPC presence and communication networks
Level maze-like structure and locked doors create implicit narrative of access control
